lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Voice Over) Previously on Friends.
[A montage of scenes from The One Where Ross and Rachel Take A Break follows.]
Monica and Rachel's, Ross is eating the picnic as Rachel comes home from work.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, I don't feel like I even have a girlfriend anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You want me to just quit my job so that you can feel like you've got a girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is this about Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (shocked) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it's not, it's not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. I cannot keep having this same fight with you Ross! Look, urrgh, maybe we should take a break.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, you're right. Let's ah, let's take a break, (goes to the door) let's cool off, okay, let's get some frozen yoghurt, or something.. (opens the door)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. A break from us.
Ross looks at her, then leaves slamming the door behind him.
Monica and Rachel's, Mark is there, opening Chinese food boxes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Then, we had this big, stupid fight, and I said I wanted to take a break, I don't want to take a break.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on the phone) I've been thinking, this is crazy, I mean don't, don't you think we can work on this?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Hey, what do you want to drink?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who's that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nobody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is that Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, honey, look he just came over to....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Got it! (slams down the phone)
The Philly.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Hey, come dance. What? Are you married? 'Cause that's okay.
[Cut to Chloe and Ross dancing, and their kiss.]
The Next Morning
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is making a fruit drink in the blender, Rachel has just finished her shower and is coming out of the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How's the big anniversary dinner?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, we never actually got to dinner.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhh, nice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, we kinda broke up instead.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! (She accidentally starts the blender without the lid on it and fruit flies everywhere.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking up at the ceiling) God, Monica it's on the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's okay, this is more important than fruit (pause) (angrily) on my ceiling! You broke up?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but it's okay, because when Ross left Mark came over.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, you and Mark?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no-no, it's okay, calm down. Mark and I talked, and I realised how much I love your stupid brother, and, yeah, we got our problems, but I really want to make it work.
Ross's Bedroom, Ross is waking up. He straightens himself out on the bed and puts the covers over his head.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: (entering from bathroom) Morning!
Ross sits bolt upright, without moving the covers.
Opening Credits
Ross's Kitchen, Ross is taking some aspirin and checking his messages.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on answering machine) Hi, it's me. I've been trying to reach you all night. I feel awful. Please, Ross, you gotta know there is nothing between me and Mark. This whole break-up thing is just stupid.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eh, I'm just so sorry I put you through it. And, I y'know, I don't want to get back together over a machine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Na-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, I love you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And y'know what, I'm gonna, I'm gonna go to bed now, but ah, on my way to work tomorrow morning, I'm gonna stop by around 8:30.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (Ross starts frantically looking for a clock.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye.
Ross finds a clock, sees it's almost 8:30, and silently screams.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chloe, Chloe how's it coming?!!

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: (entering from bedroom) Hey, what kind of puppy do you think I should get?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, oh, hey, I don't know. How about a big one?

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: But my apartment is so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well then a small one!! Listen, let's, we kinda have to get going!

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Wait! Where's my shoes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You, you sure you need shoes? (Chloe nods her head) Okay. (reaches down and picks up a shoe)

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Do I know why we're rushing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, y'know the ah, the girlfriend I told you about last night? (He's frantically throwing the cushions off of the couch looking for her other shoe) Well it turns out she ah, she wants to get back together with me. Oh, I found it!!

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: That's so great for you guys!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: You must be so happy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, yes I am, one of the many things I'm feeling. Well.... (picks up her coat)

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Good luck, with your girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank you. (She goes to kiss him, but he holds her coat up between their faces to stop her.) Hey, hey. (opens the door, sees Rachel, and hides Chloe behind the door) Rachel!!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Ohhh, you got my message.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, oh hey, you are right on time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what do you say? Can I be your girlfriend again?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, you can, very much.
Chloe gives Ross the thumbs up while still standing behind the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (seeing the thumbs up) Ahhhh!! (Hugs Rachel tighter.)
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is entering carry a large box, Monica is mopping the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, why are you mopping your ceiling?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, there's banana on it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, I have the spirit of an old Indian women living in mine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So then you know? (Phoebe nods her head)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The mailman was downstairs, so I brought up your mail.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, good. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now what is Fabutec?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, all right don't judge me to much. Okay? Um, but I saw this info-mercial, and um, I swear to you I have never-ever bought anything on TV before, except for this mop. But there was this stuff on leg waxing, it just, it looked so amazing....

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Waxine!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Have you seen it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's incredible! I so want to be a Waxine girl.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God. Do think it really doesn't hurt? 'Cause how can they do that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello! Organic substances recently discovered in the depths of the rain forest!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They have the best stuff in there.
Chandler and Joey's, Ross has told Chandler and Joey his terrible act.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. We figured when we couldn't find you, you'd gone home to make up with Rachel. Which is probably what you shoulda done. Huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You think?! God, I, ah, I'm in hell. I mean what, what am I gonna do? Rachel's all like, 'I love you and, and let's work on this.' And all I can think about is, 'What is she gonna do? What is she gonna say?' when I tell her what I did.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, before we answer that, I think we should address the more important question. How dumb are you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! Look, we're trying to rebuild a relationship here, right. How am I supposed to do that here, without being totally honest with each other?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, Ross look, I'm on board about this totally honesty thing, I am, just not about stuff that's gonna get you in trouble.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's right. Nobody's gonna benefit, and you're just gonna hurt her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and there won't be a relationship left to rebuild.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but don't you think....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right look, if you absolutely have to tell her, at least wait until the timing's right. And that's what deathbeds are for.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, okay. (he plops down into one of the leather chairs, with the footrest extended.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, okay, now, we just have to make sure she doesn't find out some other way. (spins the chair around so that Ross is facing him) Did you think about the trail?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What trail?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stomps on the footrest which pops Ross up into a sitting position) The trail from the woman you did it with to the woman you hope never finds out who did it! (slapping his hands with each word) You always have to think about the trail!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I-I don't think there's any trail.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay-okay, ah, Chloe works with that guy Issac. Issac's sister is Jasmine. And Jasmine works at that message place with Phoebe. And Phoebe's friends with Rachel. And that's the trail, I did it!
Monica's Bedroom, Monica and Phoebe are waxing their legs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (reading from the instructions) After applying the Waxine and linen strips to leg number one,

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Grasp one of the linen strips by its 'easy grab tab' and pull it off in one quick pain free motion.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (Does so.) Ow!!!!! Ow-oh-oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Was it not pain-free?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. It was painful. Oh my God , they should call it Pain-zine, now with a little wax.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh, well, the girls in the satin nighties on the commercial don't seem to think it's that bad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's because their nerves are probably deadened from being so stupid. But hey, y'know if you don't believe me, please, by my guest.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Removing one of the strips) Ow-ow-ow-ow! Oh my God!!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now, are you glad we didn't start with the bikini strips?
The Xerox Place, Ross is trying to break the trail.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Chloe? Hi..

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Is this about me taking your watch?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You took my watch?

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I do that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just you keep it, listen did you, did you tell anyone about us?

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Oh no. I feel it isn't really anybody's business, y'know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Exactly. So you didn't, didn't mention anything to Issac right?

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Oh, well I tell Issac everything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You tell, of course you do. Issac. Issac. Hey, Issac. Issac, hi! Y'know we haven't actually met...

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: You dog!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, I suppose I am a dog. But Issac, see I-I happen to have a girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Oh right, that Rachel chick from the coffee place.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, that's the one. Listen, I don't want to hurt her.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Oh, hey, man I know, doesn't matter how much we love 'em, monogamy is too cruel a rule.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Listen, can you keep this information to yourself?

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Aw, no problem dude. Y'know we got to look out for each other. We're the same, you and me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually, no, we're not.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Yeah, we are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, we're not.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Yeah, we are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, we're not!!

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Okay, we're not.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: But, we are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine. I just need to know that you're not gonna tell your sister.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: I can promise not to tell her again.
Phoebe's Work, Ross is now trying to stop Jasmine from telling Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering out of breath) Jasmine?

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We met at Phoebe's birthday party, I'm, I'm Ross Geller.

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: You did a bad thing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, I did.

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: Very bad!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Very bad.

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: Very, very bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm agreeing with you. Did you, listen, did you happen to tell Phoebe yet?

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, Jasmine, please, please don't. I love my girlfriend very much, and I want more than anything to just work it out with her. Okay?

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, thank you.

lines.speaker: Jasmine
lines.text: But you should probably talk to my roommate, because I told him and he knows Phoebe too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (angrily) Who's your roommate?
Central Perk, Gunther is wiping down the counter as Ross rushes in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gunther! Gunther. Gunther, please tell me you didn't say anything to Rachel about me and the girl from the Xerox place.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: I'm sorry. Was I not supposed to?
Ross turns around and sees Rachel sitting by the window. She is just glaring at him.
Commercial Break
Monica's Bedroom, Monica and Phoebe are removing the rest of their wax strips.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!! Ow!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ow!!!

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.text: Ow!!!!!!! Ow-ow-ow-ow!
Hearing the screaming Chandler and Joey rush in. Joey has a pan, Chandler has a tea kettle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We're all right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay, it's okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We're all right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We were just waxing our legs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Off?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: For your information, this happens to be a pain like no man will ever experience.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well I don't think you can make that statement, unless you've been kicked in an area that God only meant to be treated nicely.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I-I think that women just have a lower threshold of pain than men, that's all. I mean, come on, it's just a little wax.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, come here. (Puts a little wax on Joey's arm and puts a strip on it.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's mature.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, fine, so now what, I just pull it off?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right.
Joey laughs, pulls it off, then does a high-pitched whine.
[Cut to Living Room, Rachel is trying to close the door on Ross.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (forcing the door open) Come on, Rachel, come on! Talk to me! Please!!
[Cut to Monica's bedroom, Chandler slowly closes the door, and we hear Rachel from the living room.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't talk to you. I can't even look at you right now!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing, nothing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (going over to listen at the door) Rachel said everything was okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Joey) What, what are they talking about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel?
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just get away from me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it was a mistake! I made a mistake! Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A mistake?! What were you trying to put it in? Her purse?!
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whe-where did he put it?!
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you had sex with another woman!
[Cut to Monica's bedroom, both Monica and Phoebe gasp.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I knew something had to be wrong, because my fingernails did not grow at all yesterday.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, I guess they had a fight, and he got drunk....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh!! (hits Chandler and Joey in the head) You guys knew about this and you didn't tell us?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) He has sex, and we get hit in our heads.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (opening the door) Y'know what, I want you to leave! Get outta here!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just get out! Now!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!! No!! I wanna stay. I wanna talk about this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! All right! How was she?
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-oh.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Was she good?
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't answer that.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on Ross! You said you wanted to talk about it, let's talk about it!! How was she?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She was...
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Awful! Horrible!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She was not good. Not good.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She was nothing compared to you.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She, she was different.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ewwwww!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-oh.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good different?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nobody likes change.
Rachel picks up a newspaper and starts beating him with it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Okay, okay, okay, okay.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Should we do something?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, never cheat on Rachel.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, okay, I'm sorry. I wa-I was disgusted with myself, and this morning I was so, I was so upset and then I got your message and I was so happy, and all I wanted was to get her out of my apartment as fast as possible.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa!! Whoa, whoa, wait a minute. What time did your little friend leave? (Ross can't answer that) Oh my God. She was there? She was still there? She was in there, when I was in there?!
Ross hands Rachel back the newspaper, and she starts beating him with it again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen. Oh hey, hey, the important thing was that she meant, she meant nothing to me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And yet she was worth jeopardising our relationship!!
She throws the paper at him, misses and hits Monica's door, they all jump back at the sound.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I didn't think there was a relationship to jeopardise. I thought we were broken up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We were on a break!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That, for all I knew would, could last forever. That to me is a break-up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You think you're gonna get out of this on a technicality?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I'm not trying to get out of anything, okay. I thought our relationship was dead!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you sure had a hell of a time at the wake!
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know what, I don't think we should listen to this anymore. (Goes to open the door)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (stopping him) What, what are you doing? You can't go out there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?! (to Chandler) I'm hungry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because they'll know we've been listening.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God! And to have to hear about it from Gunther!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! Like I wanted him to tell you, I ran all over the place trying to make sure that didn't happen!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that is so sweet. I think I'm falling in love with you all over again.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, I think we can go out there. I mean they have more important things to worry about.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, we'll be fine.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look Rachel, I wanted to tell you, I thought I should, I-I did, and then Chandler and Joey convinced me not to.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (handing Monica the wax) Wax the door shut, we're never leaving, ever.
[Later, Phoebe is on the phone, they're all still trapped in Monica's bedroom.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, it's Phoebe. Listen someone's gonna have to take my 9:00 with Mr. Rehack, 'cause it's like 9:15 now, and I'm not there.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don't you realise none of this would've ever happened if I didn't think at that same moment you weren't having sex with Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. Let's say I had slept with Mark. Would you have been able to forgive me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pause) Yes I would.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You'd be okay if you knew that Mark had kissed me, and been naked with me, and made love to me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (less sure) Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You knew that our hot, sweaty, writhing bodies were....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (covering his ears and screaming) La-la-la-la-la-la-la-la-la-la! Okay, okay, yeah, I would have been devastated but, I would still want to be with you. Because it's, I mean it's you.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohhhhh!
[Later, Ross and Rachel are sitting in the kitchen.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Come on Rach, tell me what you're thinking?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm thinking, I'm gonna order a pizza.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Order a pizza like, 'I forgive you?'
She turns around and glares at him, he turns away.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh man, pizza? I like pizza. (makes like he is trying to send a telepathic message to Rachel) Put olives on the pizza.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We could eat the wax! It's organic.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh great, food with hair on it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, not the used wax.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because that would be crazy?
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, can I, can I get in on that? Because I'm kinda hungry myself.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine. (on phone) Hi! Yes, I'd like to order a large pizza.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No anchovies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: With ah, extra anchovies.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's okay, I'll just pick 'em off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and could you please chop some up and just put it right there in the sauce?
[Cut to later, they are finishing up the pizza, there's one piece left.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You can have the last piece, if you want.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I should think so. You slept with someone.
[Cut to Monica's bedroom, they're all eating the wax, Chandler and Phoebe, don't like it. Joey tries some and makes a face like: 'Hey, that's not so bad.']

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They're gonna get through this, aren't they?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, come on, it's Ross and Rachel, they've got too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What if they don't?
Long pause.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You think I need a new walk?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well y'know, I've been walking the same way since high school. Y'know, y'know how some guys they walk into a room and everybody takes notice. I think I need a 'take notice' walk.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you actually saying these words?
[Later, in the living room, Rachel is sitting on the couch, Ross is on the chair.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, now you're not even taking to me? (moves over to the coffee table) Look Rachel, I-I'm sorry, okay, I'm sorry, I was out of my mind. I thought I'd lost you, I didn't know what to do. Come on! Come on, how insane must I have been to do something like this? Huh? I-I don't cheat right, I, that's not me, I'm not Joey!
[Cut to Monica's bedroom]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-ho-ho! (He looks at Chandler, who gives him a 'come on' look.) Yeah, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. It's three in the morning. They don't know that I've come home yet. You notice how neither one of them are wondering where I am.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, y'know, people can be so self-involved.
[Cut to Living Room]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what, y'know what, I'm-I'm not the one that wanted that, that break, okay. You're the one that bailed on us. You're the one that, that ran when things got just a little rough!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's what?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is neither here nor there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, well here we are. Now we're in a tough spot again, Rach. What do you want to do? How do you want to handle it? Huh? Do you wanna fight for us? Or, do you wanna bail? (sits down next to her) Look, I, (on the verge of tears) I did a terrible, stupid, stupid thing. Okay? And I'm sorry, I wish I could take it back, but I can't. (We see Monica and Phoebe are almost in tears.) I just can't see us throwing away something we know is so damn good. Rachel, I love you so much.
He kisses her on her shoulder, then her neck, then the side of her face, then just before he kisses her on the lips....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Ross!! (stands up and moves away from him) Don't! You can't just kiss me and think you're gonna make it all go away, okay? It doesn't work that way. It doesn't just make it better. Okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (softly) I think you should go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (softly) I really think you need to go now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (moving over to stand in front of her) Okay, okay. This morning you said there was nothing so big that we couldn't work past it together...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, what the hell did I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, look, there's got to be a way we can work past this. Okay, (takes a hold of one of her arms.) I can't imagine, I can't imagine my life without you. (Both of them are starting to cry.) Without, without these arms, and your face, and this heart. Your good heart Rach, (drops to his knees and hugs her around her waist) and, and....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (crying) No. I can't, you're a totally different person to me now. I used to think of you as somebody that would never, ever hurt me, ever. God, and now I just can't stop picturing with her, I can't, (Ross stands up and backs away) it doesn't matter what you say, or what you do, Ross. It's just changed, everything. Forever.
[We see the rest of them are now crying.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (crying) Yeah, but this can't be it, I mean.
Pause

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Then how come it is?
Closing Credits
Monica's bedroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They've been quiet for a long time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe she killed him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's go.

[Cut to Living Room, Rachel is sleeping on the couch, Ross is gone, the rest of them can finally emerge from their cell. They all wave good bye, and start to walk quietly out, as Monica goes and puts a blanket on Rachel. Joey starts walking all hunched over and bobbing his shoulders as he goes.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) Is that your new walk?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (whispering) No, I really have to pee.
End
